issues: The headline 'Fair Play Award' belongs to the adjacent-column Archie McDonald column, not the Sockeyes game report. The Sockeyes article has no distinct headline in the OCR — it is a Sun News Services wire story.
The byline 'Archie McDonald' belongs to the adjacent Fair Play Award column, not the Sockeyes game report, which is attributed to 'Sun News Services'.

The short description says the game was 'in overtime' but omits that Dixon Ward scored the OT winner 59 seconds into the period — a key fact. More importantly, the draft description mixes in the Fair Play Award column content, which is from an adjacent, unrelated article.

The long description conflates two separate articles on the page: the Sockeyes wire report and Archie McDonald's Fair Play Award column about Doug Mitchell. The description should focus only on the Sockeyes game report.
The short description is otherwise factually consistent with the OCR (7-5 OT loss, 3-2 series lead), but the headline and byline are misattributed.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Red Deer Rustlers defeat Richmond Sockeyes 7-5 in overtime to take a 3-2 series lead in the tier-two junior western semi-final, with Dixon Ward scoring the winner 59 seconds into overtime.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Red Deer Rustlers beat the Richmond Sockeyes 7-5 in overtime Tuesday in Centennial Cup playoff hockey, Dixon Ward's goal 59 seconds into the 10-minute overtime period proving the winner. The victory gave Red Deer a 3-2 lead in the best-of-seven tier-two junior western semi-final series, with Game 6 scheduled for the following night in Red Deer. Sockeyes coach Orland Kurtenbach noted there was no repeat of the unruly fan behaviour that marred Sunday's match. Brian Kozak led Richmond with two goals, with Paul Rutherford, Mike Claringbull, and Jason Phillips adding singles; Phillips missed a late penalty shot before Geoff Gosselin sealed the result into an empty net.
review.notes: The page contains two distinct articles: the Sockeyes wire report (Sun News Services) and an adjacent Archie McDonald column about the Fair Play Award and Doug Mitchell pulling his junior team from a playoff series in Ontario. The AI draft incorrectly applied McDonald's byline and headline to the Sockeyes story and blended content from both articles. Corrections focus exclusively on the Sockeyes game report. No clear standalone headline is visible for the wire report in the OCR.
